title: native.reveal
description: Open the OS file browser at the given path with the target selected when the host platform supports per-item selection. Implemented over tauri-plugin-opener revealItemInDir with documented per-platform fallbacks (macOS open -R, Windows explorer.exe /select,, Linux freedesktop ShowItems with xdg-open directory fallback).

path.description: Absolute path to reveal. May point to a file or directory. Existence is checked at dispatch time, not at input-validation time.

entity_kind.description: Optional context tag for audit-log correlation.

revealed.description: True when the OS file browser launched successfully.

selection.description: How the target was highlighted. `target` means the item was selected (macOS, Windows, Linux with freedesktop ShowItems). `directory_only` means only the parent directory opened (Linux fallback). `none` means no UI hint was applied.

path.not_exists.description: The provided path does not resolve to any filesystem entry at dispatch time. User is shown a non-destructive toast and the failure is audit-logged.

os.command_failed.description: The underlying OS reveal command (open -R, explorer.exe /select,, or freedesktop ShowItems) returned a non-zero exit or D-Bus error. Audit-logged.
